meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Marketing Gaps
content.seo_description: Learn to master data-driven development. This beginner-friendly guide uses Antigravity and Claude Code to implement analytics and fix your marketing blind spots.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing degree to grow your app; you need data. By integrating robust analytics, you turn user behavior into a roadmap for product improvements. This guide teaches you how to automate your telemetry setup, ensuring every feature launch provides actionable insights rather than guessing games.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks architecting your first SaaS app, but now you face the cold, silent reality of a launch with zero feedback. The 'build it and they will come' myth is the fastest way to kill a promising project. Without eyes on your users, you are flying a plane in a thick fog, unable to see if your features actually solve the problems you intended.

But here is the engineering secret: marketing isn't just about ads; it's about data-informed iteration. By setting up a professional analytics pipeline, you transform your application into a living laboratory. You will move from being a student developer to a data-driven product builder, gaining the clarity needed to iterate your way to success.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves your user finds value (e.g., 'Project Created' or 'API Key Generated').
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest the 3 most impactful user events to track for a B2B SaaS dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Identify the primary conversion event in my current code and suggest a tracking schema.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Set up a lightweight analytics SDK that captures events without bloating your bundle size.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular analytics provider pattern using TypeScript that abstracts the tracking calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a /lib/analytics.ts file with a singleton pattern for tracking events.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Attach tracking hooks to your UI components to capture interactions without interrupting the user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook useTrackEvent that wraps my analytics provider and ensures type safety for event names.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Inject the useTrackEvent hook into my main dashboard component for tracking button clicks.'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: Connect your event stream to a dashboard so you can see trends in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dashboard query configuration to visualize user retention based on my event schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'How can I export these events to a dashboard tool like PostHog or Amplitude?'

steps.title: Validate Tracking Integrity
steps.description: Ensure your events fire correctly under edge cases like slow networks or user navigation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests to verify that analytics events are triggered on all critical path transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Write Vitest tests for the analytics service to ensure events fire on button interactions.'

pro_tips: Track 'failed' attempts as events; they are often more valuable for product discovery than successful ones.
Avoid tracking everything; focus on the 20% of interactions that drive 80% of your user value.
Use server-side tracking for mission-critical events to prevent ad-blockers from skewing your data.
Implement an 'Anonymize' flag in your SDK to remain GDPR compliant while still gathering behavioral trends.
Review your analytics dashboard weekly; data is only useful if it influences your next sprint's feature set.
content.next_steps: Once your events are flowing, schedule a weekly 'Data Review' session where you look at your funnel and identify the exact step where users drop off, then use Claude Code to run A/B tests on that specific component.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start building products people actually use. By mastering analytics now, you are building the exact skill set required by top-tier engineering teams. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
